title: Boat Rental in Amsterdam Canals: Tips for 2026
html:
Exploring the Amsterdam Canals by private boat offers a unique perspective on the city, with rentals starting from around EUR 120 for a two-hour trip. You can hire a sloop or speedboat from marinas like Amsterdam Marina or Sixhaven, and compare options on BoatHire24 to find the best deal for your group. The network of 165 canals demands a cautious approach, especially around busy junctions like the Prinsengracht.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Amsterdam Canals?

Prices range from EUR 120 for a shared tour to over EUR 2,000 for a crewed luxury yacht. A small two-person sloop costs around EUR 80, 150 per hour, while a larger motorboat for up to ten guests averages EUR 300, 500 for a half-day. For a full-day catamaran charter, expect EUR 800, 1,200. You can browse current availability and compare verified listings on boat rental Amsterdam Canals to see exact prices for 2026.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Amsterdam Canals?

In the Netherlands, boats under 15 metres and 20 km/h generally require no licence, but operators may impose their own rules. Many rental companies in Amsterdam ask for a valid photo ID and a minimum age of 21. Always confirm exact requirements with the operator before booking.

For a stress-free trip, avoid the main canals during peak hours (11:00, 15:00); instead, explore the quieter eastern side, such as the Entrepotdok area, where there is less traffic.

Where to set off in Amsterdam Canals

There are several excellent departure points around the city. Start from Sixhaven Marina in Amsterdam-Noord, which offers easy parking and direct access to the IJ. Amsterdam Marina near the NDSM wharf provides modern facilities and boat rental desks. Marinehaven in the city centre is ideal for quick trips on the Prinsengracht. Jachthaven IJburg to the east offers a calm area with less tourist traffic. Marina Amsterdam in Zeeburg is another option with good connections to the city centre by ferry or bike.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small sloop (electric), Best for couples or small families, seats 4, 6 guests, from EUR 120 per hour.
  2. Open motorboat, Ideal for groups of up to 8, with a cool box and sound system, from EUR 250 for half a day.
  3. Tender for two, Perfect for a romantic canal tour, seats 2, from EUR 80 per hour.
  4. Luxury motor yacht, The Princess V58, for example, accommodates up to 12 guests with a skipper, from EUR 1,800 per day. See the Princess V58 listing for more details.
  5. Catamaran, Spacious for 10, 12, ideal for a birthday party or corporate event, from EUR 800 for a full day.

Cost of renting a boat in Amsterdam Canals

  • Shared canal tour: EUR 20, 40 per person for one hour
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): EUR 250, 400
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): EUR 800, 1,200
  • Crewed yacht (with skipper, full-day): EUR 1,500, 2,500

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 80 to EUR 2,500 per trip
  • Peak months: May to September
  • Typical trip length: 2 to 8 hours
  • Maximum guest capacity: 12 (without licence) or up to 30 with a skipper
  • Currency: EUR
  • Licence: Not required for boats under 15 m and 20 km/h

Questions about boat rental in Amsterdam Canals

Can I rent a boat in Amsterdam without a licence?

Yes, most small rental boats do not require a licence, but you must be over 21 and provide ID.

What is the best month to visit Amsterdam for boating?

June and July offer the warmest weather and longest daylight hours, but May and September are quieter with fewer crowds.

How many people can fit on a rental boat?

Typical rental boats hold between 2 and 12 passengers; larger yachts can accommodate up to 30 with a crew.

Can I bring my own drinks and food on board?

Yes, most rental boats allow you to bring your own picnic, but check with the operator as some provide catering.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Amsterdam Canals and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
